behavior_summary: This script implements a set of bookmark/book handlers that present in-game book or log text to a player/UI by preparing an inline string, converting it to a temporary pointer, and invoking the engine Book::read routine to display it.

Each handler follows a common sequence: call an engine 'set info' on the target, create a temporary string from an inlined literal (str-to-ptr), push the current process id (pid) and the target, call Book::read with that pointer, free the temporary string, optionally spawn background tasks for deferred cleanup or side effects, then suspend/return.

The primary gameplay effect is to show flavor text (books, logs, guides) to the player when they interact with a particular bookmark/book item; spawns schedule follow-up tasks (cleanup or target methods) after initiating the read.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BOOKMARK::BOOKMARK::0080 sets object info on the provided target ([BP+06h]) then converts an embedded string literal to a heap/string pointer and immediately calls Book::read(pid, target, string_ptr).

After Book::read returns, the temporary string pointer is freed and the function issues two spawn calls: one to FREE::1C19 (likely a delayed/free worker) and one to METHOD::0676 (a method invoked on the target with arg 0x01), then the process suspends/blocks until read handling completes.

Multiple helper functions (U8P_BOOKMARK::BOOKMARK::01EB, ::0700, ::0C03, ::12CC, ::175F, ::1E2E, ::294B, ::310D, ::394D, ::4357, ::443C) replicate the same control flow pattern to present different static texts.
open_questions: Exact semantics of the 'set info' call's third indirect 2-byte argument (appears to be a type/size id) — what state or UI mode is being set on the target?
Precise behavior and timing of FREE::1C19 and METHOD::0676 spawned tasks (what cleanup or post-read side effects do they enact?)
Meaning of Book::read's return value and whether it affects subsequent logic beyond being popped/suspended (the return is captured but not used for globals here).
Whether any of these handlers are bound to particular in-game events beyond a player 'read' interaction (e.g., area triggers) in other script sections.

main_function.name: U8P_BOOKMARK::0080
main_function.purpose: Present a specific bookmarked book's text to a provided target/UI: prepare object info, allocate a temporary string from an embedded literal, call Book::read to display the text to the target/pid, free the temporary string, spawn background cleanup/side-effect tasks, then suspend/return.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player (or another actor) opens/reads the specific bookmarked book item (inferred from pattern and absence of global flag gating).
Parameter [BP+06h] is the target object/UI context receiving the book text; pid is the current process/player id implicitly pushed to Book::read.

function_map.name: U8P_BOOKMARK::0080
function_map.role: Primary bookmark/book display handler that prepares and displays a specific book text and schedules follow-up tasks.

function_map.name: U8P_BOOKMARK::01EB
function_map.role: Helper handler presenting a different long book text using the same set-info -> str-to-ptr -> Book::read -> free -> suspend pattern.

function_map.name: U8P_BOOKMARK::0700
function_map.role: Helper presenting another book text following the common pattern.

function_map.name: U8P_BOOKMARK::0C03
function_map.role: Helper presenting another volume text via Book::read.

external_calls.purpose: Attach or set preparatory metadata/state on the target object/context ([BP+06h]) before presenting the book (args include 0x0001, the target, and an indirect 2-byte value).
Convert an inlined string literal on the stack into a temporary heap/string pointer suitable for Book::read.
Display the prepared book text to the provided target/UI using the pushed pid and target; primary UI routine for book reading.
Deallocate the temporary string pointer returned by str-to-ptr after Book::read to avoid leaks.
Schedule a background cleanup or delayed-free task related to the read/display lifecycle (arguments observed: pid, 0x14, 0).
Schedule a deferred method to run on the target (arg 0x01 and the target are pushed), likely to trigger a post-read side-effect on the object.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a set of bookmark/book handlers that present in-game book or log text to a player/UI by preparing an inline string, converting it to a temporary pointer, and invoking the engine Book::read routine to display it.
